name: Chicken Biscuit Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring chicken broth to a boil; add carrots, onions, celery, mushrooms, and garlic; simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Add cubed chicken meat and peas; simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Mix cornstarch with cold water in a measuring cup; stir slowly into the chicken mixture until it reaches a gravy consistency.
4. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
5. To freeze: Pour into a round casserole dish (2-quart capacity). Cover with aluminum foil, flash freeze, then wrap in a jumbo freezer bag.
6. To prepare: Thaw the casserole before cooking. Place it in a 400-degree oven for 25 minutes.
7. Remove from oven and place uncooked biscuits (either from "tube" or scratch—my family enjoys their "chicken biscuits" cut with a chicken-shaped cookie cutter) in slightly-overlapping circles on top of the casserole.
8. Return to oven and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, until bubbling and biscuits are cooked.
Serving Ideas: Fruit salad busted by sooz
